title: 100% Waterproof SPC Flooring in Gurgaon & Delhi NCR
metatitle: 100% Waterproof SPC Flooring in Gurgaon | Best Supplier - Goals Floors
shortDescription: Looking for the best SPC flooring in Gurgaon? Goals Floors is your top supplier and dealer for 100% waterproof luxury wooden flooring. Built with a rock-solid stone core, it never gets damaged by water, scratches, or termites, making it perfect for homes and offices across Delhi NCR.
longDescription: Goals Floors is the premier supplier and dealer of SPC (Stone Plastic Composite) Flooring in Gurgaon, Delhi, and Noida. Upgrade to the future of wooden flooring with our Cobra Gold SPC Series. If you want the luxury aesthetic of real wood but fear water damage, dampness (seelan), or termites (deemak), SPC is your ultimate permanent solution. Unlike traditional wooden laminate floors that swell up when wet, our SPC flooring is built with a rock-solid stone core, making it completely 100% waterproof and termite-proof. Designed specifically for the harsh climate and busy lifestyle of Delhi NCR, it features a heavy-duty 0.5mm protective wear layer that effortlessly handles heavy foot traffic in corporate offices, retail stores, and pet-friendly homes. Enjoy the stunning look of natural hardwood with zero maintenance. It installs directly over your existing tiles in just 1 to 2 days without any messy cement or expensive demolition.

features.title: 100% Waterproof & Termite Proof
features.description: The ultra-dense stone core absorbs absolutely zero moisture. It will never swell, warp, or attract termites (deemak), making it the safest wooden flooring option for ground floors, basements, and modular kitchens in Delhi NCR facing dampness (seelan) issues.

features.title: Ultra-Tough Top Layer (0.5mm Commercial Grade)
features.description: Built specifically for heavy commercial traffic. This thick 0.5mm transparent shield protects the floor's design from deep scratches, high heels, moving office furniture, and active pet claws.

features.title: Feels Like Real Wood (EIR Technology)
features.description: We use advanced EIR (Embossed In Register) 3D printing technology, ensuring the physical texture matches the wood grain design perfectly. It doesn't just look like expensive hardwood; it feels exactly like it underfoot.

features.title: Fast, Glue-Free Installation
features.description: Engineered with a smart 'Uniclick' system. The heavy SPC planks seamlessly lock together like a precision puzzle without needing any messy adhesives, cement, or toxic glues, ensuring a zero-odor installation.

features.title: Premium Painted V-Groove
features.description: Each SPC plank features carefully painted, slightly beveled edges. This micro V-groove gives your completed floor the highly authentic, expensive architectural look of individual hardwood planks.

features.title: Quiet & Fire Safe
features.description: Comes with an integrated, sound-absorbing foam underlay pad so it does not produce a hollow clicking sound when walking. The stone composite core also makes it highly fire-retardant for maximum family safety.

applications: Luxury Living Rooms & Master Bedrooms in Gurgaon
Modern Modular Kitchens & Dining Areas (100% Waterproof)
Corporate Offices & Executive Cabins in Cyber City
installation: SPC installation in Gurugram is completely dry, fast, and dust-free. You do not need to break your old floor. As long as your existing tiles, marble, or concrete subfloor is flat and clean, we simply click the rigid SPC planks together over the integrated acoustic pad. Custom color-matching SPC skirting and T-profiles are securely added at the walls and doorways for a flawless, professional finish in just 24 to 48 hours.
maintenance: SPC flooring requires almost zero maintenance. You can sweep or vacuum it daily to remove dust. Because the rigid stone core is 100% waterproof, you can freely use a daily wet mop (pocha) with normal mild floor cleaners. To maintain the 0.5mm wear layer for decades, simply avoid throwing buckets of water or using harsh wire scrubbers.

faqs.question: What is an SPC flooring?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring is a rigid vinyl flooring made from a Stone Plastic Composite core that combines limestone powder, stabilizers, and PVC for strength and durability.

SPC flooring is designed to be waterproof, impact-resistant, and easy to maintain. It is commonly used in homes, offices, retail stores, and commercial spaces because it offers the appearance of wood or stone without the maintenance requirements of natural materials.

faqs.question: Is SPC fully waterproof?
faqs.answer:
Yes, SPC flooring is fully waterproof and can withstand spills, moisture, and humidity without swelling or warping.

Unlike laminate or traditional wooden flooring, SPC does not absorb water. This makes it suitable for kitchens, basements, and areas prone to moisture. In many Delhi NCR homes, SPC flooring is chosen for spaces where occasional water exposure is a concern.

faqs.question: What are the disadvantages of SPC flooring?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring has a few disadvantages despite its durability and waterproof performance.

• It can feel harder underfoot than WPC flooring.
• Lower-quality products may produce more sound when walked on.
• Damaged planks usually need replacement rather than repair.

Choosing a quality underlay and premium-grade SPC flooring can help reduce these issues.

faqs.question: How long will SPC flooring last?
faqs.answer:
Quality SPC flooring typically lasts between 15 and 25 years when properly installed and maintained.

Its lifespan depends on wear layer thickness, foot traffic, installation quality, and maintenance practices. Commercial-grade SPC flooring may last even longer in moderate-use environments.

faqs.question: Is SPC flooring expensive?
faqs.answer:
No, SPC flooring is generally considered a mid-range flooring option and is often more affordable than hardwood flooring while offering premium performance.

• Basic SPC flooring: ₹120–₹180 per sq. ft.
• Mid-range SPC flooring: ₹180–₹300 per sq. ft.
• Premium SPC flooring: ₹300–₹500+ per sq. ft.

Installation costs may be additional depending on the project.

faqs.question: Which is better, SPC or laminate?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring is generally better than laminate flooring in areas exposed to moisture because it is fully waterproof.

• SPC offers superior water resistance.
• Laminate often feels warmer and softer underfoot.
• SPC typically provides better durability in humid environments.

For many projects in Gurugram and Delhi NCR, SPC is preferred because it performs better during humid weather and accidental spills.

faqs.question: Is SPC flooring slippery?
faqs.answer:
No, most SPC flooring products are designed with textured wear layers that provide good slip resistance under normal conditions.

However, like most flooring materials, the surface can become slippery if excessive water, oil, or other liquids are present. Choosing anti-slip finishes can further improve safety.

faqs.question: Is SPC flooring better than tile?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring is often considered better than tile for comfort and installation speed, while tiles may offer greater heat resistance and longevity in some applications.

• SPC feels warmer and more comfortable underfoot.
• Tiles are highly resistant to scratches and heat.
• SPC installation is typically faster and cleaner.

The best choice depends on the space, budget, and performance requirements.

faqs.question: Which is better SPC or wooden flooring?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring is generally better for moisture resistance and maintenance, while natural wooden flooring is often preferred for its premium appearance and authenticity.

• SPC is waterproof and easy to maintain.
• Wood offers natural beauty and higher resale appeal.
• SPC performs better in humid environments.

In areas where seelan or moisture can be a concern, SPC flooring is often the more practical option.

faqs.question: Which is cheaper, SPC or WPC?
faqs.answer:
SPC flooring is generally cheaper than WPC flooring for comparable quality levels.

• SPC typically has a lower material cost.
• WPC usually feels softer and more cushioned underfoot.
• SPC offers greater rigidity and impact resistance.

While SPC is often the more economical option, WPC may provide additional comfort in residential applications.

installationSteps.title: Check the Old Floor
points: Ensure your existing floor is completely flat and level.
Sweep and clean to ensure it is dust-free.
No moisture issues should be present.
installationSteps.tip: A flat floor prevents the planks from bending when you walk on them.

installationSteps.title: Lay the Acoustic Underlay
points: Roll out the premium sound-absorbing foam sheet (IXPE/EVA).
Use tape to seal the seams so the foam doesn't move.
This makes walking quiet and comfortable.

installationSteps.title: Click & Lock the Planks
installationSteps.description: Install without any glue or cement:
points: Take an SPC plank, angle it at 30 degrees.
Insert it into the groove of the previous plank.
Press it down firmly to lock it in place seamlessly.
Use a small tapping block to ensure the joints are invisible.

installationSteps.title: Maintain Wall Gaps
points: Leave a small gap (around 1/4 inch) near all the walls.
This gap is covered later by wooden skirting.
It allows the building to naturally shift without buckling the floor.
